ogTitle: Greta Main vs Grevillia: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Greta Main and Grevillia.
heading: Greta Main vs Grevillia — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Greta Main, New South Wales and Grevillia, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Greta Main has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Grevillia (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Greta Main or Grevillia safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Greta Main has a flood risk score of 24/100, while Grevillia scores 44/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Greta Main scores 40/100 for bushfire risk. Grevillia scores 32/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Greta Main or Grevillia?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Greta Main has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.48% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
